# UC-23.3.2 · Lead-to-Revenue Funnel Conversion Rates

## Description

Tracks conversion rates at every stage of the marketing-sales funnel — visitor to lead, lead to MQL, MQL to SQL, SQL to opportunity, opportunity to closed-won. Identifies where the biggest leaks are and whether marketing is delivering quality leads that sales can close — the perennial question in every marketing-sales alignment meeting.

## Value

Tracks conversion rates at every stage of the marketing-sales funnel — visitor to lead, lead to MQL, MQL to SQL, SQL to opportunity, opportunity to closed-won. Identifies where the biggest leaks are and whether marketing is delivering quality leads that sales can close — the perennial question in every marketing-sales alignment meeting.

## Implementation

(1) Import lead and opportunity records via DB Connect; (2) map your CRM status values to the standard funnel stages; (3) schedule weekly; (4) alert when any stage conversion rate drops below historical baseline; (5) segment by lead source, geography, and product interest for actionable insights.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=business` `sourcetype="dbx:crm_leads"` and `sourcetype="dbx:crm_opportunities"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Import lead and opportunity records via DB Connect; (2) map your CRM status values to the standard funnel stages; (3) schedule weekly; (4) alert when any stage conversion rate drops below historical baseline; (5) segment by lead source, geography, and product interest for actionable insights.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=business sourcetype="dbx:crm_leads" earliest=-90d
| eval status_norm=lower(status)
| stats dc(lead_id) as total_leads,
        dc(eval(if(match(status_norm,"qualified|mql|marketing.qualified"),lead_id,null()))) as mqls,
        dc(eval(if(match(status_norm,"sql|sales.qualified|accepted"),lead_id,null()))) as sqls,
        dc(eval(if(isnotnull(converted_opportunity_id),lead_id,null()))) as opportunities,
        dc(eval(if(match(status_norm,"closed.won|won"),lead_id,null()))) as closed_won
| eval lead_to_mql=round(100*mqls/total_leads, 1)
| eval mql_to_sql=round(100*sqls/mqls, 1)
| eval sql_to_opp=round(100*opportunities/sqls, 1)
| eval opp_to_won=round(100*closed_won/opportunities, 1)
| eval overall=round(100*closed_won/total_leads, 2)
| table total_leads, mqls, lead_to_mql, sqls, mql_to_sql, opportunities, sql_to_opp, closed_won, opp_to_won, overall
```

Understanding this SPL

**Lead-to-Revenue Funnel Conversion Rates** — Tracks conversion rates at every stage of the marketing-sales funnel — visitor to lead, lead to MQL, MQL to SQL, SQL to opportunity, opportunity to closed-won. Identifies where the biggest leaks are and whether marketing is delivering quality leads that sales can close — the perennial question in every marketing-sales alignment meeting.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=business` `sourcetype="dbx:crm_leads"` and `sourcetype="dbx:crm_opportunities"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: business; **sourcetype**: dbx:crm_leads. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=business, sourcetype="dbx:crm_leads", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **status_norm** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **lead_to_mql** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **mql_to_sql** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **sql_to_opp** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **opp_to_won** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **overall** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Pipeline stage (see **Lead-to-Revenue Funnel Conversion Rates**): table total_leads, mqls, lead_to_mql, sqls, mql_to_sql, opportunities, sql_to_opp, closed_won, opp_to_won, overall


Step 3 — Validate
Compare stage conversion rates to the marketing automation platform, the CRM dashboard, and the web analytics dashboard (GA, Adobe) for the same funnel definition.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Funnel chart, Single values (conversion rate per stage), Line chart (weekly conversion trends), Bar chart (conversion by lead source).

## SPL

```spl
index=business sourcetype="dbx:crm_leads" earliest=-90d
| eval status_norm=lower(status)
| stats dc(lead_id) as total_leads,
        dc(eval(if(match(status_norm,"qualified|mql|marketing.qualified"),lead_id,null()))) as mqls,
        dc(eval(if(match(status_norm,"sql|sales.qualified|accepted"),lead_id,null()))) as sqls,
        dc(eval(if(isnotnull(converted_opportunity_id),lead_id,null()))) as opportunities,
        dc(eval(if(match(status_norm,"closed.won|won"),lead_id,null()))) as closed_won
| eval lead_to_mql=round(100*mqls/total_leads, 1)
| eval mql_to_sql=round(100*sqls/mqls, 1)
| eval sql_to_opp=round(100*opportunities/sqls, 1)
| eval opp_to_won=round(100*closed_won/opportunities, 1)
| eval overall=round(100*closed_won/total_leads, 2)
| table total_leads, mqls, lead_to_mql, sqls, mql_to_sql, opportunities, sql_to_opp, closed_won, opp_to_won, overall
```

## Visualization

Funnel chart, Single values (conversion rate per stage), Line chart (weekly conversion trends), Bar chart (conversion by lead source).
